title: Chest Auscultation
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- As part of a normal patient assessment: 
- Particularly when the patient has a respiratory complaint.
- To confirm placement of advanced airways.

#### Procedure

- Position the patient in an upright seated position. 
- Only if conscious and if there are no spinal precautions or signs of shock.

- Explain the procedure to the patient, ensuring they understand to take deep breaths in and out of their mouth. 
- Nose breathing causes distorted perception of breath sounds.
- Do not attempt to listen to breath sounds through clothing, bone, breast tissue or excessive fat tissue.

- Place the stethoscope over the bases, and work towards the apexes. 
- A methodical and horizontal approach is best in order to compare the right and left (see **Figure 1**).

- Make note of the location and type of any abnormal breath sounds.

- On completion inform the patient that they can resume normal breathing.

Figure 1
